name: Removable Defects: The Economics and Limits of Deliberate Deficiency
summary: arXiv:2607.11983v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: A specialist tolerates blind spots that a generalist does not. Usually this is treated as a cost to be minimized. We treat it as a design v...
description: arXiv:2607.11983v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: A specialist tolerates blind spots that a generalist does not. Usually this is treated as a cost to be minimized. We treat it as a design variable: a deficiency can be kept because it pays and removed on demand in the rare situation where it would be fatal, by routing to a compensation channel. We give three results. First, an advantage...
author: Cheng Qian
